behavior_summary: This script implements a simple 'U8P_NSHOLLOG::look' (examine) handler for a hollow-log object. When the player uses the U8P_NSHOLLOG::look/examine action on the object, the process emits a single short text line: "hollow log ", then suspends/ends the U8P_NSHOLLOG::look process (likely waiting for player acknowledgement).
No branching, menu interactions, state mutations, or external calls are present; it is purely descriptive output for the object's examine action.
notable_control_flow: U8P_NSHOLLOG::look executes linearly: display a bark (text) then suspend/terminate the process.
There are no conditional branches or loops — the function always performs the same single output.
open_questions: The emitted text contains a trailing space ("hollow log "). Is that intentional formatting or an extraction artifact?

There are two low-confidence 'Unknown' nodes in the raw data (samples '{' and 'suspend'). Are these merely structural/decompiler remnants or do they alter timing/flow beyond the visible bark + suspend?

main_function.name: U8P_NSHOLLOG::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text shown when the player examines the hollow-log object.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the game's 'U8P_NSHOLLOG::look' (examine) action for the object (process type 0x0208).
Also conceptually triggered by any engine event that invokes the object's U8P_NSHOLLOG::look handler.

function_map.name: U8P_NSHOLLOG::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_NSHOLLOG::look handler that emits a single descriptive line and suspends.
